title: Mapo Tofu Vegetarian

seo.title: Authentic Vegetarian Mapo Tofu Recipe (Easy!)
seo.metaDescription: Craving authentic Chinese flavors? Try our easy Vegetarian Mapo Tofu recipe! This spicy, savory dish is packed with flavor and perfect for a meatless meal. Get the full recipe now!
longTailKeywords: how to make Vegetarian Mapo Tofu
Vegetarian Mapo Tofu step by step
best Vegetarian Mapo Tofu at home
h2Headings: How to Make Vegetarian Mapo Tofu: Step-by-Step
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant bowl of authentic Vegetarian Mapo Tofu, garnished with green onions, ready to be served.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, spicy, and savory taste of this authentic Vegetarian Mapo Tofu – a perfect meatless meal.

description: Experience the fiery allure of Sichuan cuisine with our Vegetarian Mapo Tofu, a plant-based delight that doesn't compromise on flavor. This iconic dish features silken tofu cubes bathed in a rich, spicy, and savory sauce, elevated by the signature numbing sensation of Sichuan peppercorns. It's a comforting and robust meal perfect for any occasion, proving that vegetarian dishes can be incredibly bold and satisfying.

ingredients.notes: white and light green parts, thinly sliced, divided

instructions.title: Prepare the Tofu and Sichuan Peppercorns
instructions.text: Gently press the tofu to remove excess water, then cut into 3/4-inch cubes. Blanch the tofu in boiling water for 2-3 minutes, then drain and set aside. This helps firm the tofu and removes any raw taste. In a dry wok or pan, toast the whole Sichuan peppercorns over medium heat for 2-3 minutes until fragrant. Remove, cool slightly, then grind them into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder. Set aside.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics and Pastes
instructions.text: Heat vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the minced garlic, grated ginger, and the white and light green parts of two scallions. Stir-fry for about 1 minute until fragrant. Add the vegetarian Doubanjiang and fermented black beans. Stir-fry for another 2-3 minutes, pressing the paste against the wok to release its oils and aroma, until the oil turns reddish.

instructions.title: Simmer the Sauce
instructions.text: Pour in the vegetable broth, light soy sauce, and sugar. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, stirring to combine all the flavors. Taste the sauce and adjust seasonings if necessary; it should be savory, spicy, and slightly sweet.

instructions.title: Add Tofu and Thicken
instructions.text: Carefully add the blanched tofu cubes to the simmering sauce. Gently stir to coat the tofu without breaking it. Reduce the heat to medium-low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the tofu to absorb the flavors. While the tofu simmers, mix the cornstarch with water in a small bowl to create a smooth slurry.

instructions.title: Finish the Dish
instructions.text: Stir the cornstarch slurry once more, then slowly pour it into the simmering tofu mixture while stirring constantly. Continue stirring until the sauce thickens to your desired consistency. Remove from heat. Drizzle with sesame oil and sprinkle about half of the ground Sichuan peppercorns over the top.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Transfer the Vegetarian Mapo Tofu to a serving bowl. Garnish generously with the remaining sliced scallions and the rest of the ground Sichuan peppercorns. Serve immediately with steamed white rice for a truly authentic Chinese meal.

tips: Always use authentic Pixian Doubanjiang for the most genuine flavor, ensuring it's a vegetarian version. Its deep umami and spice are irreplaceable.
Blanching the tofu before adding it to the sauce helps firm up the texture and allows it to absorb the flavors better, preventing it from crumbling.
Adjust the amount of Sichuan peppercorns to your preference for the 'ma la' (numbing and spicy) sensation. For extra heat, add a dash of chili oil at the end.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop or in the microwave.
Serve with plain steamed white rice to balance the rich and spicy flavors of the Mapo Tofu.
article: Mapo Tofu Vegetarian is a beloved Chinese Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
